channel.title: AI Builders Digest
channel.description: Daily AI builders digest - bilingual EN/ZH

item.title: AI Builders Digest · 2026-03-27
item.description:
AI Builders Digest — March 27, 2026 北京时间 2026年3月27日
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ X / TWITTER ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Former Tesla/OpenAI AI Director Andrej Karpathy flagged a subtle but important UX problem with LLM personalization: memory features are actively distracting models. When a single unrelated fact (e.g. "I like cats") is stored, it tends to bleed into every response — and this pattern appears across all major LLMs, not just one implementation. His observation: "A single question could be the memory about 'I like cats' and suddenly the AI is putting cats in every response." This suggests the industry still hasn't solved contextual relevance in personalization. https://x.com/karpathy/status/2036841069636370467 https://x.com/karpathy/status/2036836816654147718
Andrej Karpathy（前 Tesla/OpenAI AI 总监）指出了 LLM 个性化中一个微妙但重要的 UX 问题：记忆功能正在干扰模型。当一个不相关的事实（比如"我喜欢猫"）被存储后，它往往会渗透到每一条回复中——而且这个现象几乎出现在所有主流 LLM 上，并非某个具体实现的问题。这说明整个行业在个性化中的"上下文相关性"问题上仍未解决。 https://x.com/karpathy/status/2036841069636370467 https://x.com/karpathy/status/2036836816654147718
---
Vercel CEO Guillermo Rauch made a sharp observation about the economics of AI: "Every company will become an AI factory, wherein the unit of production is the token." But he's not just bullish — he flags the tension this creates: tokens generate usage trails, creating a potential data liability. Companies will need to rethink both their production model and their data governance simultaneously. https://x.com/rauchg/status/2036963706576527623
Vercel CEO Guillermo Rauch 对 AI 经济学作出了一个精准判断："每家公司都将成为 AI 工厂，生产的基本单位是 token。"但他并非只是看多——他同时指出了由此产生的张力：token 会留下使用轨迹，形成潜在的数据责任。企业需要同时重构生产模式和数据治理体系。 https://x.com/rauchg/status/2036963706576527623
---
Box CEO Aaron Levie is watching Jevons paradox play out in real time with AI. As AI lowers the cost of knowledge work, companies — especially outside tech — are realizing they can now afford tasks they previously couldn't. Rather than reducing total AI spend, lower costs are expanding overall demand. "Companies are now asking: what else can we do?" https://x.com/levie/status/2036832183131033977
Box CEO Aaron Levie 正在实时观察 AI 领域的杰文斯悖论。随着 AI 降低了知识工作的成本，企业——尤其是非科技行业——开始意识到他们现在可以承担以前负担不起的任务。成本下降不但没有减少 AI 总支出，反而在扩大整体需求。"企业们都在问：我们还能做什么？" https://x.com/levie/status/2036832183131033977
---
YC President & CEO Garry Tan argued that the new era demands aggressive token usage: "You have to use tokens aggressively to create something remarkable." He's essentially saying that the builders who win will be the ones willing to throw compute at problems rather than optimizing for frugality. The cost curve has shifted enough that the constraint is imagination, not tokens. https://x.com/garrytan/status/2037055498974093629
YC 总裁兼 CEO Garry Tan 主张，新时代要求积极地使用 token："你必须积极地消耗 token，才能创造出真正出色的东西。"他的意思是：赢家将是那些愿意用算力砸问题的人，而不是那些追求极度节省的人。成本曲线已经下移到足够低——限制你的是想象力，而不是 token。 https://x.com/garrytan/status/2037055498974093629
---
Cat Wu, who works on Claude Code at Anthropic, shared that "Auto mode is a step change improvement in the Claude Code UX, balancing autonomy and safety. Almost everyone on our team uses it now." This is a significant signal — when the team building the product switches to a new mode en masse, it's a meaningful product moment, not just a feature launch. https://x.com/_catwu/status/2036852880624541938
Anthropic Claude Code 团队成员 Cat Wu 分享道，"Auto 模式是 Claude Code 用户体验的一次阶跃式提升，在自主性和安全性之间取得了平衡。我们团队几乎所有人现在都在用它。"这是一个重要信号——当打造产品的团队自己集体切换到新模式时，这不只是一个功能发布，而是一个有实质意义的产品时刻。 https://x.com/_catwu/status/2036852880624541938
---
Thariq (Claude Code, Anthropic) announced that iMessage is now available as a channel in Claude Code, alongside a pointer to the docs. This continues Anthropic's push to make Claude Code accessible through more communication surfaces — not just the CLI or web UI. https://x.com/trq212/status/2036959638646866021
Anthropic Claude Code 团队的 Thariq 宣布 iMessage 现已作为 Claude Code 的一个新渠道上线，并附上了文档链接。这延续了 Anthropic 让 Claude Code 覆盖更多通信入口的推进方向——不再局限于 CLI 或 Web UI。 https://x.com/trq212/status/2036959638646866021
---
Cursor AI Designer Ryo Lu pushed back on the "AI replaces design" narrative with a nuanced counter: "As agents make it easy to add features, design matters more, not less. The role is no longer just pushing pixels — it's deciding what to build and what not to build." When generating features becomes trivial, curation and judgment become the scarce resource. https://x.com/ryolu_/status/2036886854805709097
Cursor AI 设计师 Ryo Lu 用一个细致的反驳回应了"AI 取代设计"的叙事："随着 agent 让添加功能变得容易，设计反而变得更重要，而不是更不重要。这个角色不再只是像素搬运工——而是决定该做什么、不该做什么。"当生成功能变得轻而易举，筛选和判断才是稀缺资源。 https://x.com/ryolu_/status/2036886854805709097
---
Every CEO Dan Shipper articulated the shift happening in product development: "PMs and designers can ship software directly. Engineers can build at 10x speed. The rules of professional product development are being rewritten in real time." He's teasing something big coming from Every tomorrow — worth watching. https://x.com/danshipper/status/2036827118915485942
Every CEO Dan Shipper 描述了产品开发正在发生的转变："PM 和设计师可以直接交付软件。工程师的构建速度提升了 10 倍。专业产品开发的规则正在实时被重写。"他还预告 Every 明天将有大动作——值得关注。 https://x.com/danshipper/status/2036827118915485942
---
FirstMark VC Matt Turck offered a sardonic but pointed take on how VC board behavior has evolved: "2016: governance, guidance, support. 2021: cheerleaders. 2026: Anthropic reseller." It's a wry comment on how much VCs now center their value-add narrative around AI tooling access rather than operational expertise. https://x.com/mattturck/status/2036747586468499889
FirstMark VC Matt Turck 对 VC 董事会行为的演变作出了一个讽刺而犀利的总结："2016 年：治理、指导、支持。2021 年：啦啦队。2026 年：Anthropic 经销商。"这是对 VC 们如何将自身价值叙事从运营专长转向 AI 工具访问权的辛辣评论。 https://x.com/mattturck/status/2036747586468499889
---
Replit CEO Amjad Masad shared a positive signal for AI-generated apps: Apple appears to be accepting Replit-built apps at a high rate. For anyone betting on vibe coding as a path to app distribution, this is meaningful — the App Store gatekeeping hasn't been a blocker. https://x.com/amasad/status/2037004600893472936
Replit CEO Amjad Masad 分享了一个对 AI 生成应用的积极信号：苹果似乎正以较高通过率接受 Replit 构建的应用。对于押注"vibe coding"作为应用分发路径的人来说，这很有意义——App Store 的把关并没有成为障碍。 https://x.com/amasad/status/2037004600893472936
---
Linear Head of Product Nan Yu reflected on the dilution of the word "design": "I really wish we could reclaim design back to meaning 'a plan for arranging elements to accomplish a particular purpose.'" As AI democratizes aesthetics, the original systems-thinking meaning of design becomes more valuable — and more ignored. https://x.com/thenanyu/status/2037042617213481410
Linear 产品负责人 Nan Yu 对"设计"这个词被滥用表达了思考："我真的希望我们能重新把'设计'的含义拉回到'为实现特定目的而安排要素的计划'。"随着 AI 让美学民主化，设计最初的系统性思维含义变得更有价值——也更被忽视。 https://x.com/thenanyu/status/2037042617213481410
---
Anthropic's Claude account announced that Claude's work tools (Figma, Canva, Amplitude, and more) are now available on mobile. This is a distribution move — meeting users where they already are and reducing the friction for on-the-go professional workflows. https://x.com/claudeai/status/2036850783526719610
Anthropic 的 Claude 官方账号宣布，Claude 的工作工具（Figma、Canva、Amplitude 等）现已在移动端上线。这是一次分发层面的举措——在用户已经活跃的地方迎接他们，降低移动端专业工作流的摩擦。 https://x.com/claudeai/status/2036850783526719610
---
Peter Yang (creator/educator) went from "got a squat rack" to vibe-coding his own fitness app in the same breath — a fun illustration of where we are: building personal software for your own use cases is now as casual as setting up gym equipment. https://x.com/petergyang/status/2037029503177023705
Peter Yang（创作者/教育者）在同一条推里从"终于搞了个深蹲架"直接跳到"那就顺手用 vibe coding 做个健身 app 好了"——这是一个有趣的缩影：为自己的需求开发软件，现在已经和安装健身器材一样随意了。 https://x.com/petergyang/status/2037029503177023705
---
South Park Commons GP Aditya Agarwal observed from Hill Valley Forum: "It takes three to tango for hardtech/deeptech: Builders + Investors + Government." The note highlights that deep tech has a different capital and partnership structure than pure software — and that forum brought all three together in the same room. https://x.com/adityaag/status/2036824253970923925
South Park Commons 普通合伙人 Aditya Agarwal 从 Hill Valley Forum 带回观察："硬科技/深科技需要三方共舞：创业者 + 投资人 + 政府。"这一点揭示了深科技与纯软件不同的资本和合作结构——而这个论坛将三方汇聚在了同一个房间里。 https://x.com/adityaag/status/2036824253970923925
---
OpenClaw founder Peter Steinberger announced a new OpenClaw beta with better Microsoft Teams integration and OpenWebUI support. For the persistent-agent crowd, Teams is a significant enterprise surface — and OpenWebUI gives a self-hosted chat UI option. https://x.com/steipete/status/2036824286988816737
OpenClaw 创始人 Peter Steinberger 宣布了新版 OpenClaw beta，带来了更好的 Microsoft Teams 集成和 OpenWebUI 支持。对于持久 agent 生态来说，Teams 是一个重要的企业级入口——而 OpenWebUI 则提供了一个自托管聊天界面选项。 https://x.com/steipete/status/2036824286988816737
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ PODCASTS ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
🎙 Training Data — "Biology's Waymo Moment: Ginkgo Bioworks' Jason Kelly" https://youtube.com/watch?v=g45Alfg7diw
**The Takeaway:** AI is finally doing something that no previous tech wave could — it's becoming load-bearing infrastructure for biology and pharma, not just back-office IT.
Ginkgo Bioworks CEO Jason Kelly makes a striking argument: every major tech revolution before AI — the internet, social media, mobile — was essentially irrelevant to biotech and biopharma. "Yeah it's nice, we communicate slightly better, whatever — it's just some back-office IT crap." AI is different. For the first time, software is directly interfacing with the core work of biology: reading genomes, designing proteins, automating lab workflows.
Kelly draws a direct parallel to autonomous vehicles. Just as Waymo spent years building the underlying tech stack before meaningful deployment, biology is now at its own "Waymo moment" — the infrastructure is reaching the threshold where real-world application becomes viable at scale. The bottleneck shifts from "can we do this?" to "how fast can we deploy?"
What makes this compelling for builders: Ginkgo isn't just a biotech company, it's a platform play. They're building the cell programming infrastructure that other companies can build on top of — an AWS analogy for synthetic biology. Kelly sees AI as the unlock that finally makes this platform economically viable, because it dramatically reduces the cost of the design-build-test cycle that is the core loop of all biology work.
The counterintuitive insight: the reason prior tech didn't matter to biotech was that the bottleneck was never communication or information management — it was the ability to actually design and manipulate biological systems. AI addresses exactly that bottleneck.
---
**要点：** AI 终于做到了以前任何技术浪潮都无法做到的事——它正在成为生物学和医药领域真正的基础设施，而不仅仅是后台 IT 工具。
Ginkgo Bioworks CEO Jason Kelly 提出了一个令人印象深刻的论点：在 AI 之前，每一次重大技术革命——互联网、社交媒体、移动互联网——对生物科技和生物制药来说基本上都是无关紧要的。"是挺好的，我们沟通稍微顺畅一点，但就这样——不过是些后台 IT 的小把戏。"AI 不同。这是第一次，软件直接介入生物学的核心工作：读取基因组、设计蛋白质、自动化实验室流程。
Kelly 直接类比了自动驾驶汽车。就像 Waymo 花了多年时间打造底层技术栈，才得以真正部署，生物学现在正处于自己的"Waymo 时刻"——基础设施正在逼近阈值，大规模实际应用变得切实可行。瓶颈从"我们能做到吗？"转移到"我们能多快落地？"
对创业者来说，这里有个值得关注的视角：Ginkgo 不只是一家生物科技公司，它是一个平台游戏。他们在构建细胞编程基础设施，让其他公司可以在上面搭建业务——类似合成生物学领域的 AWS。Kelly 认为 AI 是最终让这个平台在经济上可行的解锁钥匙，因为它大幅降低了"设计-构建-测试"循环的成本，而这个循环正是所有生物学工作的核心。
反直觉的洞见：以往技术对生物科技无关紧要的原因，并不是因为沟通或信息管理不重要——而是因为真正的瓶颈从来都是：我们能不能实际设计和操控生物系统。AI 恰恰解决的就是这个瓶颈。
https://youtube.com/watch?v=g45Alfg7diw
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

item.title: AI Builders Digest · 2026-03-26
item.description:
AI Builders Digest — March 26, 2026
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ X / TWITTER ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
⚠️ Former Tesla/OpenAI AI Director Andrej Karpathy issued an urgent security warning: the litellm Python package was the victim of a supply chain attack. A simple `pip install litellm` was enough to silently exfiltrate SSH keys, AWS/GCP/Azure credentials, Kubernetes configs, git credentials, environment variables, and all API keys. This is a serious wake-up call for any developer with AI dependencies in their stack. https://x.com/karpathy/status/2036487306585268612
⚠️ 前特斯拉/OpenAI AI 总监 Andrej Karpathy 发出紧急安全警告：litellm Python 包遭遇供应链攻击。仅仅执行 `pip install litellm` 就足以悄悄窃取 SSH 密钥、AWS/GCP/Azure 凭证、Kubernetes 配置、git 凭证、环境变量和所有 API key。对于在技术栈中使用 AI 依赖库的开发者来说，这是一记严重的警钟。 https://x.com/karpathy/status/2036487306585268612
---
OpenAI CEO Sam Altman says AI will help discover new science — including cures for diseases — which he considers the most important long-term lever for improving quality of life. He also acknowledged that AI will present new threats to society. In a lighter moment, he's looking for a single word to describe the feeling of "throwing everything into the model with as much context as possible" — a mood every power user will recognize. https://x.com/sama/status/2036488680769241223 https://x.com/sama/status/2036489823792607273
OpenAI CEO Sam Altman 表示，AI 将帮助发现新科学，包括治愈疾病，他认为这是长期提升人类生活质量最重要的方式。他同时也承认 AI 会给社会带来新威胁。轻松一刻：他想找一个词来描述"把所有东西和尽可能多的上下文一股脑扔给模型"这种感觉——每个重度用户都懂这个心情。 https://x.com/sama/status/2036488680769241223 https://x.com/sama/status/2036489823792607273
---
Anthropic's Claude account announced Claude Code's new "auto mode" — now available as a research preview on the Team plan, rolling out to Enterprise and API soon. Instead of making users approve every file write and bash command (or dangerously skipping permissions), auto mode has a classifier review each tool call before execution: safe actions proceed automatically, risky ones get blocked. This is a meaningful step toward agentic AI that's actually production-safe. https://x.com/claudeai/status/2036503582166393240 https://x.com/claudeai/status/2036503583667933400 https://x.com/claudeai/status/2036503584943034643
Anthropic 的 Claude 官方账号宣布 Claude Code 推出"auto mode"——目前作为研究预览版向 Team 计划开放，Enterprise 和 API 访问即将推出。不再需要用户审批每次文件写入和 bash 命令（也不用危险地跳过权限），auto mode 通过分类器在执行前审查每次工具调用：安全操作自动执行，高风险操作被拦截，Claude 另寻他路。这是 agent AI 走向真正可生产部署的重要一步。 https://x.com/claudeai/status/2036503582166393240 https://x.com/claudeai/status/2036503583667933400 https://x.com/claudeai/status/2036503584943034643
---
Anthropic researcher Alex Albert put it simply: "Goodbye --dangerously-skip-permissions, hello auto mode." The new flag is `--enable-auto-mode`, and you can cycle to it with Shift+Tab. The community has clearly been waiting for this. https://x.com/alexalbert__/status/2036510206155432293
Anthropic 研究员 Alex Albert 简洁总结道："再见 --dangerously-skip-permissions，你好 auto mode。"新的启用方式是 `--enable-auto-mode`，按 Shift+Tab 可以切换到该模式。社区显然已经等待这个功能很久了。 https://x.com/alexalbert__/status/2036510206155432293
---
Thariq, a Claude Code engineer at Anthropic, noted with some irony: "turns out being an AI safety company is useful for when you need to make sure AIs can run safely." He also previewed a Claude Code × Figma livestream on March 31st and teased a feature that currently only works on Teams but is being scaled. https://x.com/trq212/status/2036513038983995820 https://x.com/trq212/status/2036515949617037711 https://x.com/trq212/status/2036442894777594248
Anthropic Claude Code 工程师 Thariq 带着点讽刺意味说道："原来做 AI 安全公司，在需要确保 AI 能安全运行的时候真的很有用。" 他还预告了 3 月 31 日的 Claude Code × Figma 直播，并透露了一个目前仅限 Teams 版本、正在扩大规模的功能。 https://x.com/trq212/status/2036513038983995820 https://x.com/trq212/status/2036515949617037711 https://x.com/trq212/status/2036442894777594248
---
Box CEO Aaron Levie delivered a pointed observation for software vendors: "If you're building software that can't work fully headlessly in a way that agents want to use, you're not future-proof." He's quoting a company that's already moved off legacy systems with poor, slow, and inconsistent APIs — a preview of what agentic workflows will demand from every SaaS stack. https://x.com/levie/status/2036478998667207012
Box CEO Aaron Levie 对软件厂商发出了一个尖锐的警告："如果你构建的软件无法以 agent 期望的方式完全无头运行，那你就没有未来。" 他引用了一家已经从慢速、过时、不一致 API 的遗留系统迁移出去的公司——这预示着 agent 工作流将对每个 SaaS 产品栈提出的要求。 https://x.com/levie/status/2036478998667207012
---
YC President & CEO Garry Tan pushed back on the "AI will replace developers" framing: "Once you say 'replace developers' it's the wrong ideaspace. Cars didn't replace walking — you still have to walk a little bit AND it let people go to places you can't go to on foot alone." He also teased that AI will make his output 90x what it was in 2013. https://x.com/garrytan/status/2036674371855823153 https://x.com/garrytan/status/2036675789819953347
YC 总裁兼 CEO Garry Tan 反驳了"AI 将取代开发者"的论调："一旦你说'取代开发者'，你的思维框架就错了。汽车没有取代步行——你还是得走一点路，同时它让人们能去靠步行到不了的地方。" 他还暗示 AI 将让他的产出达到 2013 年的 90 倍。 https://x.com/garrytan/status/2036674371855823153 https://x.com/garrytan/status/2036675789819953347
---
Vercel CEO Guillermo Rauch shared two sharp signals. First: Vercel now intelligently picks the right hardware for each build, adapting to the new wave of Rust-based compilers (Turbopack, Rolldown) that scale with CPU count. Second and more striking: "Almost every SaaS app inside Vercel has now been replaced with a generated app or agent interface, deployed on Vercel" — covering support, sales, marketing, PM, HR, data viz, design, and video workflows. He's eating his own cooking. https://x.com/rauchg/status/2036584870995173678 https://x.com/rauchg/status/2036447879985037495
Vercel CEO Guillermo Rauch 分享了两个清晰的信号。其一：Vercel 现在能智能选择每次构建的合适硬件，适配以 Turbopack、Rolldown 为代表的新一代 Rust 编译器（这些编译器的性能随 CPU 数量线性扩展）。其二，更震撼：「Vercel 内部几乎每个 SaaS 应用，现在都已被生成式应用或 agent 界面取代，部署在 Vercel 上」——覆盖支持、销售、市场、PM、HR、数据可视化、设计和视频工作流。他正在践行自己的理念。 https://x.com/rauchg/status/2036584870995173678 https://x.com/rauchg/status/2036447879985037495
---
Linear Head of Product Nan Yu shared a frank reflection: "I haven't written a PRD by hand, filed an issue through a form, or hand-written any code in months. But the volume of work I'm producing and the quality bar have never been higher." His incentive for making agents work? Simply not wanting to do annoying work anymore. https://x.com/thenanyu/status/2036549647267709110 https://x.com/thenanyu/status/2036631571118084476
Linear 产品负责人 Nan Yu 坦率分享了自己的体会："我已经好几个月没有手写过 PRD、没有通过表单提交过 issue、也没有手写过任何代码了。但我的产出量和质量标准从未像现在这么高。" 他推动 agent 发挥作用的动力？就是不想再做那些烦人的重复工作了。 https://x.com/thenanyu/status/2036549647267709110 https://x.com/thenanyu/status/2036631571118084476
---
Roblox Product lead Peter Yang sees AI agents replacing the entire document-driven workflow: specs, designs, issues, roadmaps, strategy docs, spreadsheets — all of it. His hope is that AI handles the paperwork so teams can obsess over the actual end-user product. He also noted with amusement that his Telegram is now entirely composed of chats with AI bots — not a single human conversation. https://x.com/petergyang/status/2036621615086309468 https://x.com/petergyang/status/2036664048700600706
Roblox 产品负责人 Peter Yang 认为 AI agent 将取代整个文档驱动的工作流：需求文档、设计稿、issue、路线图、战略文档、表格——全部。他希望 AI 处理这些文书工作，让团队能专注于真正的用户产品。他还有些好笑地发现，他的 Telegram 现在全是和 AI bot 的对话——没有一个真人。 https://x.com/petergyang/status/2036621615086309468 https://x.com/petergyang/status/2036664048700600706
---
FPV Ventures partner Nikunj Kothari turned a security compliance observation into a data project: he scraped the subprocessor lists of 417 companies to map which AI infrastructure is actually being used in production by companies like Notion, Ramp, Brex, Harvey, Perplexity, and Evidence. A rare look at real B2B AI adoption through the lens of legal compliance docs. https://x.com/nikunj/status/2036572222081606065 https://x.com/nikunj/status/2036573898289086895
FPV Ventures 合伙人 Nikunj Kothari 把一个安全合规观察变成了数据项目：他爬取了 417 家公司的 subprocessor（数据处理方）列表，绘制出 Notion、Ramp、Brex、Harvey、Perplexity 等公司在生产环境中实际使用的 AI 基础设施地图。这是通过法律合规文件窥探真实 B2B AI 采用情况的罕见视角。 https://x.com/nikunj/status/2036572222081606065 https://x.com/nikunj/status/2036573898289086895
---
Builder and creator Zara Zhang crossed 13K GitHub stars — remarkable for someone who says she "barely knew how GitHub worked" a year ago. She reflected: "Something shifted in January." She's also shipping: frontend-slides now supports deploying decks as shareable URLs and exporting to PDF. Her take: "GitHub is the new Substack. Code is a form of self-expression." https://x.com/zarazhangrui/status/2036552689673445847 https://x.com/zarazhangrui/status/2036590721927618988 https://x.com/zarazhangrui/status/2036675965003411514
创作者 Zara Zhang 在 GitHub 上累积了 13K+ star——令人印象深刻，尤其是她说自己一年前"几乎不懂 GitHub 怎么用"。她反思道："一月份发生了什么转变。" 与此同时她也在持续输出：frontend-slides 现在支持将演示文稿部署为可分享的 URL，并导出为 PDF。她的观点："GitHub 是新的 Substack，代码是一种自我表达。" https://x.com/zarazhangrui/status/2036552689673445847 https://x.com/zarazhangrui/status/2036590721927618988 https://x.com/zarazhangrui/status/2036675965003411514
---
South Park Commons GP and ex-Dropbox CTO Aditya Agarwal sees a clear near-term unlock for robotics: "The hardware is ready. We just need to get the software to a point where it is reliable and scalable." He's bullish on the US as the right country to win this. https://x.com/adityaag/status/2036426693930291334
South Park Commons GP、前 Dropbox CTO Aditya Agarwal 对机器人领域看到了清晰的近期机遇："硬件已经就绪，我们只需要让软件达到可靠且可扩展的程度。" 他看好美国在这场竞赛中胜出。 https://x.com/adityaag/status/2036426693930291334
---
swyx (AI Engineer, Latent Space) shared enthusiasm for Devin's ability to catch its own mistakes — a meta-capability he says saves him 3–8x effort daily. He also noted that Sora appears to be "the first casualty of OpenAI's crackdown on Side Quests." https://x.com/swyx/status/2036565584515899445 https://x.com/swyx/status/2036533647659143630
AI 工程师 swyx (Latent Space) 对 Devin 能捕捉自身错误的能力表示赞叹——他说这个元能力每天帮他节省 3-8 倍的工作量。他还指出，Sora 似乎是"OpenAI 打压副业项目的第一个牺牲品"。 https://x.com/swyx/status/2036565584515899445 https://x.com/swyx/status/2036533647659143630
---
Google Labs VP Josh Woodward says Google is hiring across Gemini App, Google AI Studio, and Google Labs: "There's never been a more exciting time. The place is pulsating." https://x.com/joshwoodward/status/2036513009661780291
Google Labs VP Josh Woodward 表示 Google 正在 Gemini App、Google AI Studio 和 Google Labs 全面招聘："从来没有哪个时刻比现在更令人兴奋，整个地方都在涌动。" https://x.com/joshwoodward/status/2036513009661780291
---
FirstMark VC Matt Turck offered a dry observation on the cultural shift around typos: "Typo, before: you did sloppy work! Typo today: you wrote authentic, non-AI generated content! That's awesome." https://x.com/mattturck/status/2036488357077717094
FirstMark VC Matt Turck 对打字错误的文化意涵变化做出了一个幽默的观察："以前发现错别字：你太粗心了！今天发现错别字：你写的是真人内容、没有 AI 代劳！太棒了。" https://x.com/mattturck/status/2036488357077717094
---
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ PODCASTS ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Latent Space — "There Is No AlphaFold for Materials: AI for Materials Discovery with Heather Kulik" https://youtube.com/watch?v=KSCCKCz2x04
The Takeaway: LLMs are surprisingly bad at the precise atomic-level design tasks that materials scientists actually care about — and that gap is exactly where specialized AI models shine.
MIT Chemical Engineering professor Heather Kulik has spent her career at the intersection of AI and materials science, long before it was fashionable. She runs an accelerated discovery lab focused on finding new materials with computational and data-driven methods. The episode is a grounding counterpoint to the hype: where general-purpose LLMs like ChatGPT have "PhD-level Wikipedia knowledge" of chemistry, they consistently fail at tasks like "design me a ligand with exactly 22 atoms" — a trivially precise ask for a working chemist. Kulik's research shows that the real value of AI in materials science isn't replacing chemists, it's surfacing surprising predictions — things a very smart chemist wouldn't have already known. Her group has made major advances in applying machine learning to transition metal complexes and molecular design, using AI not just to speed up known calculations but to find genuinely non-obvious candidates. She's also deeply thoughtful about the workflow of AI-assisted science: how you validate outputs, when to trust predictions, and why "the thing that sounds weird is often the thing worth exploring." For anyone building or investing in scientific AI, this episode is a useful dose of technical nuance from someone who's been doing the hard work for years.
Latent Space ——「材料领域没有 AlphaFold：与 Heather Kulik 探讨 AI 在材料发现中的应用」 https://youtube.com/watch?v=KSCCKCz2x04
核心洞察：LLM 在材料科学家真正关心的精确原子级设计任务上出奇地差——而这个差距恰恰是专用 AI 模型大放异彩之处。
MIT 化学工程学教授 Heather Kulik 早在这一方向流行之前，就将职业生涯押注于 AI 与材料科学的交叉领域。她的加速发现实验室专注于用计算和数据驱动方法寻找新材料。这期播客是对过度炒作的一次有益校正：通用 LLM 如 ChatGPT 在化学领域有「PhD 水准的维基百科知识」，但在「帮我设计一个恰好含 22 个原子的配体」这种对工作中的化学家来说极其基础的精准任务上，它们却屡屡失败。Kulik 的研究表明，AI 在材料科学中的真正价值不是取代化学家，而是挖掘出让人意外的预测——那些即便是顶尖化学家也未必已知的发现。她的团队在将机器学习应用于过渡金属配合物和分子设计方面取得了重大进展，AI 的作用不仅仅是加速已知的计算，而是找到真正非显而易见的候选方案。她对 AI 辅助科研的工作流程也有深刻思考：如何验证输出、何时信任预测、以及为什么「听起来奇怪的东西往往最值得探索」。对于正在构建或投资科学 AI 的人来说，这期节目是来自多年深耕者的宝贵技术洞见。

item.title: AI Builders Digest · 2026-03-25
item.description:
AI Builders Digest — March 25, 2026 (Beijing Time)
═══════════════════════════════════════ X / TWITTER ═══════════════════════════════════════
───────────────────────────────────── Anthropic Researcher Alex Albert ───────────────────────────────────── Anthropic researcher Alex Albert shared a striking data point: theoretical physicist Matthew Schwartz found Claude Opus 4.5 to be roughly at the level of a second-year grad student, and it accelerated his research by 10x. Albert also noted that the future where you never have to open a laptop to get work done is arriving faster than expected. https://x.com/alexalbert__/status/2036232980059062550 https://x.com/alexalbert__/status/2036227208675729687
Anthropic 研究员 Alex Albert 分享了一个令人印象深刻的数据点：理论物理学家 Matthew Schwartz 发现 Claude Opus 4.5 大约达到了二年级研究生的水平，将他的研究速度提升了 10 倍。Albert 还提到，「不需要打开笔记本电脑就能完成工作」的未来正在以超乎预期的速度到来。 https://x.com/alexalbert__/status/2036232980059062550 https://x.com/alexalbert__/status/2036227208675729687
───────────────────────────────────── Claude Code Engineer Thariq ───────────────────────────────────── Claude Code engineer Thariq dropped a fun stat: Anthropic acquired a computer use company and shipped computer use in Claude Code just 4 weeks later. Computer control is now live in Claude Code Desktop. https://x.com/trq212/status/2036302910498173034 https://x.com/trq212/status/2036199619416178814
Claude Code 工程师 Thariq 透露了一个有趣的细节：Anthropic 收购了一家 computer use 公司，仅 4 周后就在 Claude Code 中上线了电脑控制功能。这项功能目前已在 Claude Code Desktop 版本中正式可用。 https://x.com/trq212/status/2036302910498173034 https://x.com/trq212/status/2036199619416178814
───────────────────────────────────── Claude (Official) ───────────────────────────────────── The official Claude account announced a major new capability: you can now assign tasks from your phone and come back to finished work on your computer. Claude uses connected apps (Slack, Calendar, etc.) first, and when no connector exists, asks permission to control the app directly on screen. Available on Pro and Max plans. https://x.com/claudeai/status/2036195795100123446 https://x.com/claudeai/status/2036195792101212445
Claude 官方账号宣布了重大新功能：你现在可以在手机上分配任务，回来时电脑上的工作已经完成。Claude 优先使用已连接的 app（Slack、日历等），如果没有对应连接器，会请求权限直接控制屏幕上的应用。功能在 Pro 和 Max 计划中可用。 https://x.com/claudeai/status/2036195795100123446 https://x.com/claudeai/status/2036195792101212445
───────────────────────────────────── Box CEO Aaron Levie ───────────────────────────────────── Box CEO Aaron Levie argues that computer use and the ability to write and run code on the fly are the ultimate primitives for agents tackling enterprise knowledge work. Most work requires hopping between multiple applications with broad datasets — agents need to traverse these systems to automate any real workflow. https://x.com/levie/status/2036290915950797314
Box CEO Aaron Levie 认为，电脑控制能力和即时编写/运行代码的能力是 agent 在企业知识工作中发挥作用的终极基础能力。大多数工作都需要在多个应用之间切换并处理大量数据，agent 必须能够跨越这些系统才能真正实现自动化。 https://x.com/levie/status/2036290915950797314
───────────────────────────────────── YC President & CEO Garry Tan ───────────────────────────────────── YC CEO Garry Tan just submitted his first-ever PR to the Y Combinator internal codebase — a 2,400-line change into a 1.84 million line repo with 14 years of technical debt — using GStack/Conductor. His key insight: GStack's skills transfer even to giant, messy production codebases, not just greenfield projects. https://x.com/garrytan/status/2036330844516917297 https://x.com/garrytan/status/2036330296308802012
YC 总裁兼 CEO Garry Tan 刚刚向 Y Combinator 内部代码库提交了他有史以来第一个 PR——在一个拥有 14 年技术债的 184 万行代码库中改动了 2400 行，使用的是 GStack/Conductor。他的核心发现：GStack 的能力不只适用于从零开始的项目，对于庞大、混乱的生产代码库同样有效。 https://x.com/garrytan/status/2036330844516917297 https://x.com/garrytan/status/2036330296308802012
───────────────────────────────────── OpenAI VP of Science Kevin Weil ───────────────────────────────────── OpenAI VP of Science Kevin Weil highlighted a young astronomer who used AI to analyze massive datasets and identified 1.5 million new celestial objects in space — earning a NASA job offer and a fighter jet ride as a signing bonus. Weil sees this as a sign of AI's transformative potential in science. https://x.com/kevinweil/status/2036095876628693168
OpenAI 科学副总裁 Kevin Weil 介绍了一位用 AI 分析海量数据集的年轻天文学家，他发现了 150 万个新的天体——NASA 因此向他抛出橄榄枝，还附赠了一次战斗机试飞体验。Weil 认为这正是 AI 在科学领域变革性潜力的体现。 https://x.com/kevinweil/status/2036095876628693168
───────────────────────────────────── FirstMark VC Matt Turck ───────────────────────────────────── FirstMark VC Matt Turck put the Claude Code moment in historical perspective: it's going to be hard to explain to future generations that there was once a time when people with an idea would spend years searching for a "technical co-founder." https://x.com/mattturck/status/2036182407779365309
FirstMark 风险投资人 Matt Turck 从历史角度看待 Claude Code 的意义：未来的人们将很难理解，曾经有一个时代，有想法的人需要花费数年时间寻找「技术联合创始人」。 https://x.com/mattturck/status/2036182407779365309
───────────────────────────────────── Linear Head of Product Nan Yu ───────────────────────────────────── Linear's head of product Nan Yu shared a remarkable moment: Codex noticed a prompt included a line that obliquely referenced a feature hidden behind a feature flag elsewhere in the app — and flagged it. His question: what percentage of human reviewers would catch this? https://x.com/thenanyu/status/2036095568733143258
Linear 产品负责人 Nan Yu 分享了一个令人震惊的瞬间：Codex 发现他的 prompt 里有一行代码隐晦地引用了 app 其他地方被 feature flag 隐藏的功能，并且主动标记了出来。他的问题是：有多少人类 reviewer 能发现这个问题？ https://x.com/thenanyu/status/2036095568733143258
───────────────────────────────────── Swyx (Latent Space / AI Engineer) ───────────────────────────────────── Swyx analyzed the Dreamer x Meta exec hire on the 9-month anniversary of Zuck's "Personal Superintelligence" manifesto, touching on Vibes, Manus, and what MSL Apps will have to show by year end. He's also taking predictions on what Meta's AI lab will acquire next. Separately, he noted a pattern of Latent Space / AI Engineer podcast alumni getting major investment from Scale AI's Alexandr Wang and NAT. https://x.com/swyx/status/2036337043475931340 https://x.com/swyx/status/2036205006806982931
Swyx 在 Zuck 发布《Personal Superintelligence》宣言 9 个月纪念日之际，分析了 Dreamer x Meta 高管收购事件，涉及 Vibes、Manus，以及 MSL Apps 到年底要交出什么成绩单。他还在征集关于 Meta AI 实验室下一步会收购谁的预测。另外，他注意到一个规律：Latent Space / AI Engineer 播客的嘉宾频频获得 Scale AI Alexandr Wang 和 NAT 的重磅投资。 https://x.com/swyx/status/2036337043475931340 https://x.com/swyx/status/2036205006806982931
───────────────────────────────────── Sam Altman (OpenAI CEO) ───────────────────────────────────── Sam Altman announced he is stepping down from the Helion board as OpenAI and Helion begin exploring working together at significant scale. He remains a financial stakeholder in Helion (and will continue to recuse himself from negotiations) but says the governance situation requires the move. He remains very bullish on Helion and the future of abundant energy. https://x.com/sama/status/2036137695605563682
Sam Altman 宣布从 Helion 董事会辞职，原因是 OpenAI 和 Helion 开始探索大规模合作。他仍持有 Helion 的财务利益（在谈判中继续回避），但表示治理层面的考量需要他做出这个决定。他对 Helion 和能源丰盛未来的信念丝毫未减。 https://x.com/sama/status/2036137695605563682
───────────────────────────────────── Peter Steinberger (CodexBar / AI Tools) ───────────────────────────────────── Peter Steinberger shared his evolving PR review workflow with Codex: flag issues, assess clarity, ask if this is the best possible fix (95% of the time it isn't), then continue discussion before rewriting. He also released CodexBar 0.19.0 with Alibaba Coding Plan support, subscription history charts, and improved Claude stability. https://x.com/steipete/status/2036252448399171717 https://x.com/steipete/status/2036245531522113910
Peter Steinberger 分享了他结合 Codex 的 PR review 工作流：标记问题，判断是否清晰，追问这是否是最佳修复方案（95% 的情况下不是），然后继续讨论再重写。他还发布了 CodexBar 0.19.0，新增阿里云编码计划支持、订阅历史图表和 Claude 稳定性改进。 https://x.com/steipete/status/2036252448399171717 https://x.com/steipete/status/2036245531522113910
───────────────────────────────────── Every CEO Dan Shipper ───────────────────────────────────── Every CEO Dan Shipper coined a useful nuance: "if you can vibe code it you can vibe fix it — but you can't always vibe fix it quickly." The asymmetry matters as AI-generated codebases grow in complexity. https://x.com/danshipper/status/2036146776319680624
Every CEO Dan Shipper 提出了一个有用的观察："如果能 vibe code，就能 vibe fix——但 vibe fix 不一定很快。"随着 AI 生成的代码库越来越复杂，这种不对称性正在变得重要。 https://x.com/danshipper/status/2036146776319680624
───────────────────────────────────── Replit CEO Amjad Masad ───────────────────────────────────── Replit CEO Amjad Masad highlighted a viral TSA wait time app built entirely on Replit — a neat example of real consumer utility emerging from AI-assisted vibe coding. https://x.com/amasad/status/2036272008158433564
Replit CEO Amjad Masad 展示了一款在 Replit 上构建的病毒式传播的 TSA 等待时间 app——这是 AI 辅助 vibe coding 催生真实消费者价值的一个有趣案例。 https://x.com/amasad/status/2036272008158433564
───────────────────────────────────── Peter Yang (Creator Economy / AI Tutorials) ───────────────────────────────────── Creator and AI educator Peter Yang praised Anthropic for shipping basically every day, calling it the bar for what AI-native development looks like. He also reflected on college admissions becoming harder — and why persistence in building beats gatekeepers. https://x.com/petergyang/status/2036212646962434395
创作者和 AI 教育者 Peter Yang 盛赞 Anthropic 几乎每天都在发布新功能，称这是 AI 原生开发的标杆。他也分享了关于大学录取竞争加剧的思考——以及为什么坚持构建有人需要的东西终将胜过守门人。 https://x.com/petergyang/status/2036212646962434395
───────────────────────────────────── FPV Ventures Partner Nikunj Kothari ───────────────────────────────────── FPV Ventures partner Nikunj Kothari noted the SF startup bubble in miniature: every conversation circles back to Jevons paradox, Goodhart's law, and the bitter lesson. His take: read more history, art, and sci-fi. https://x.com/nikunj/status/2036108908532801630
FPV Ventures 合伙人 Nikunj Kothari 观察到旧金山的一个小圈子现象：每次对话都会绕回到 Jevons 悖论、Goodhart 定律和 bitter lesson。他的建议：多读历史、艺术和科幻。 https://x.com/nikunj/status/2036108908532801630
───────────────────────────────────── Google Labs ───────────────────────────────────── Google Labs announced its experimental virtual try-on app Doppl will shut down on April 30, 2026. The underlying virtual try-on technology will live on across Google product listings and image results — another Labs experiment graduating to core product. https://x.com/GoogleLabs/status/2036188820128071786
Google Labs 宣布其实验性虚拟试穿 app Doppl 将于 2026 年 4 月 30 日停止运营。底层虚拟试穿技术将继续在 Google 商品列表和图片搜索结果中提供——这是又一个从 Labs 实验毕业进入核心产品的案例。 https://x.com/GoogleLabs/status/2036188820128071786
═══════════════════════════════════════ PODCASTS ═══════════════════════════════════════
No new podcast episodes today.
今日无新播客节目。
═══════════════════════════════════════ 🍊 小橙子 · AI Builders Digest Generated: 2026-03-25 (Beijing Time) ═══════════════════════════════════════

item.title: AI Builders Digest - 2026-03-24
item.description:
🗞 AI Builders 日报 · 2026.03.24
🐦 X / Twitter
① Thariq Shihipar · Claude Code @ Anthropic
Anthropic acquired a computer use company, then shipped the feature in Claude Code just 4 weeks later. Also flagged a new capability currently Desktop-only.
Anthropic 收购了一家 computer use 公司，4 周后直接集成进 Claude Code 上线。还透露有个新能力目前只有 Desktop 版才有。
🔗 x.com/trq212/status/2036302910498173034
② Aaron Levie · CEO @ Box
Computer use + the ability to write & run code are the "ultimate primitives" for agents. Simple framing, but cuts right to why agentic AI differs from chat AI.
computer use 和代码生成/运行是 agent 承接知识工作的两个底层 primitive。切中了 agentic AI 和 chat AI 的核心区别。
🔗 x.com/levie/status/2036290915950797314
③ Alex Albert · Research @ Anthropic
Highlighted a physicist's post on how Claude Opus 4.5 is accelerating real research. Also: "The future where I never have to open my laptop is becoming real very fast."
分享了物理学家用 Opus 4.5 加速科研的文章。本人感慨：「不需要打开电脑就能完成工作的未来，来得比想象快得多。」
🔗 x.com/alexalbert__/status/2036232980059062550
④ Nan Yu · Head of Product @ Linear
Codex picked up a subtle, oblique reference in a prompt that a human reviewer might have missed. His take: "Sometimes you have to step back and realize how insane this is."
Codex 从 prompt 里捕捉到连人类可能漏掉的暗示并据此行动。感慨：「有时候你需要停下来，意识到这有多疯狂。」
🔗 x.com/thenanyu/status/2036095568733143258
⑤ Matt Turck · VC @ FirstMark Capital
"Going to be hard to explain to future generations that there was a time when people spent years on something Claude Code can now do."
「很难跟未来的人解释，Claude Code 几分钟搞定的事，曾经要花好几年。」
🔗 x.com/mattturck/status/2036182407779365309
⑥ Swyx · AI researcher / founder
Analysis of the Dreamer x Meta exec hire — timed to the 9-month anniversary of Zuck's "Personal Superintelligence" memo. Worth reading for big-lab talent watchers.
分析「Dreamer x Meta 高管引进」事件，结合 Zuck 备忘录 9 个月节点解读。关注大厂人才争夺战的可以看。
🔗 x.com/swyx/status/2036337043475931340
⑦ Sam Altman · CEO @ OpenAI
Shared that he loved being on the Helion board and remains "extremely excited" about a future of abundant energy.
对 Helion 任期回顾满满，对能源充裕的未来依然「极度兴奋」。
